overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted professional presence across Bluesky, Mastodon, and LinkedIn, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. While LinkedIn commands the largest follower base and highest absolute volume of interactions, Bluesky exhibits the strongest relative audience engagement, proving to be an exceptionally receptive hub for academic publishing and networking.

contentThemes.description: Research findings, paper publications, and presentations focused on detecting coordinated sharing and information operations.
Promoting career milestones, research grants, and active recruitment for postdoctoral and PhD vacancies.
Technical updates on open-source software like CooRnet and discussions regarding multi-platform methodological approaches.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high engagement rate to cross-promote hiring and grant achievements, which currently perform well on LinkedIn.
Increase visual asset usage, such as sharing screenshots of CooRnet v2.0 or diagrams from newly published papers, to further drive click-throughs.
Maintain consistent multi-platform posting, particularly utilizing Mastodon for highly technical research discussions and LinkedIn for institutional accomplishments.

platformInsights.bluesky: Acts as the most interactive channel with an average engagement rate of 6.14%. Academic paper releases and PhD opportunities stimulate high repost rates here, reflecting a highly active scholarly community.
platformInsights.mastodon: Serves as a specialized tech-and-academic niche. While follower counts are lower, technical updates (e.g., CooRnet v2.0) generate consistent engagement (4.55% average rate), proving its value for targeted developer and researcher outreach.
platformInsights.linkedin: Represents the largest reach channel (1,850 followers) driving significant absolute engagement (4.86% average rate). It is highly effective for broadcasting institutional milestones, research grants, and formal job openings.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
